ocr_mistral.text:
SINGLE AFFIDAVIT
FOR GENERAL PURPOSES IN UNITED STATES CLAIM BUSINESS.

A NEW RULE.

The Departments of Washington have made a new rule, requiring where a witness signs his or her name to be affidavit by making his or her mark (a), that two persons who can write must attest such statement by signing on the left side of the name of the officer making a mark. For The Officer administering the oath can not be one of the attesting parties. Thus, no example:

Attest:
JOHN BROWN.
JOSEPH SMITH.

Sworn to, subscribed, &c.,

HENRY HART.
AMOS RIPLEY,
Justice of Peace, &c.

State of Kentucky County of Henderson SS.

On this 21st day of August A.D. 1867, before me H. S. Park, a Notary Public within and for the County and State aforesaid, personally came and appeared
Roy Chambers, aged Sixty-six years, a resident of the County of
Henderson in the State of Kentucky, who, being by me duly sworn according to law, on oath declares that she is the true and lawful widow of Aaron Chambers, decd, late a Private Company G 118th U.S.C.T. Infantry, who died in field Hospital State of Virginia on the sixth day of November 1864, from effect of disarrival, incised in his line of duty.

She further declares she is unable to obtain a certificate from the Surgeon of this Regiment or Company Officer for the reason that they were all mother men and do not reside in the community of her residence and she cannot get the place of their residence or Post Office address, and in lieu of the abovenamed certificate she herewith sends the affidavit of two of his fellow soldiers as to the cause and place of this death, it being the best evidence she is able to obtain.

She further swears she cannot send Record proof of her marriage with the abovementioned soldier, for the reason that the law of Kentucky did not require a license & a record for the marriage of persons of color but recognized them as man and wife from the act of cohabitation and he (Aaron) & her lived together as man and wife according to the custom in Kentucky for persons of color, in proof of which she herewith sends the affidavit of two disinterested witnesses.

her
Robert C. Chambers
mark

Attest
R. C. Thornton
J. L. Weller

Sworn to and subscribed before me, this 21st day of August in the year 1867, and I hereby certify that the affiant is a respectable and credible person and resides as stated; that I believe statements are correct and worthy of full faith and credit; that I have no interest in this matter. I further certify that the foregoing affidavit was read over, fully explained to, and understood by the affiant before the signing and execution thereof, and also that the affiant is the identical party represented as making the affidavit.

H. S. Park
N. P. H. C. K.
